chunks.text: EECS Safety Manual - EECS at Berkeley EECS Safety Manual Faculty, staff, and students are encouraged to return to their offices, labs, and other research spaces without occupancy or distancing restrictions. Everyone is responsible for staying curren

tions. Everyone is responsible for staying current on the latest campus safety guidelines . Cory and Soda Building Access Requirements Complete the Workplace Safety Training program through the UC Learning Center (estimated time: 30 minutes). If you

arning Center (estimated time: 30 minutes). If you are unable to login to the Workplace Safety Training, please review the Workplace Safety Training PDF version and proceed to Step 2. Review the Building Emergency Plans for Cory or Soda Hall to learn

ing Emergency Plans for Cory or Soda Hall to learn emergency evacuation routes and procedures. Review the EECS Department Safety Contacts (below) so you know whom to contact before an actual emergency. Submit an EECS Safety Compliance Form to confirm

. Submit an EECS Safety Compliance Form to confirm that you have completed the above steps. If you need card key access or a metal door key, submit an EECS Card Key/Metal Key Request Form . You must complete steps 1-4 above before submitting this req

omplete steps 1-4 above before submitting this request. After completing the EECS Card Key/Metal Key Request Form, if you need to pick up a physical door key (in addition to card key activation) for Cory or Soda, schedule a time for an EECS Metal Key

ory or Soda, schedule a time for an EECS Metal Key Pick-Up . If you have applied for access and your card key isn’t working, submit an EECS Cardkey Access Problems Form . Questions? keys@eecs.berkeley.edu EECS Department Safety Contacts Emergency Hot
